stories.title: Aaron Rai Wins PGA Championship — 290-1 Longshot Makes History
stories.angle: The ultimate underdog story: a quiet English golfer who wears two gloves, was ranked outside the top 50 entering the week, and won a major on Sunday in a manner nobody saw coming.

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
AARON RAI wins the 2026 PGA Championship. 🏆

Odds on Thursday morning: 290-1.
Final round: 65 (-5).
Final score: -9. Winner by 3.

First Englishman to win the Wanamaker Trophy since 1919.

Nobody had him. He had everyone.

#GolfFanrecap #PGAChampionship

article.headline: Aaron Rai Wins the PGA Championship: The 290-1 Longshot Who Rewrote History at Aronimink

stories.title: McIlroy's Grand Slam Dream Denied Again at Aronimink
stories.angle: McIlroy's Grand Slam pursuit remains the golf story that refuses to resolve. At 36, the window continues to narrow — though his game is clearly still capable of the challenge.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Rory McIlroy's Grand Slam quest comes up short. Again.

T7 at Aronimink. Two birdies. Bogey at 13. Final round 69.

He was in contention. He needed to go low. He couldn't.

The PGA Championship still eludes him. 😔

#GolfFanrecap #McIlroy #PGAChampionship

article.headline: McIlroy's Grand Slam Dream Denied Again: What Went Wrong at Aronimink

stories.title: Smalley's Heartbreak — Led at 54 Holes, Fell to T2
stories.angle: Smalley's story is both heartbreaking and encouraging — a player without a PGA Tour win who held the 54-hole lead at a major. The talent is undeniable. The major win will come.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Alex Smalley had the 54-hole lead.

Double-bogey 6. Bogey 8. He fell back.

Still finished T2 at -6 alongside Jon Rahm. LIV Golf star Rahm shoots 68 Sunday.

The major is still elusive for Smalley. The talent is not.

#GolfFanrecap #PGAChampionship

article.headline: Smalley's Heartbreak and Rahm's Resilience: Two Stories Behind the PGA Championship's T2

stories.title: CJ Cup Byron Nelson Preview — Scheffler Returns to Defend
stories.angle: A 144-player field attacks one of the most birdie-friendly courses on the PGA Tour. Scheffler is the defending champion and the world's No. 1 player. Can anyone challenge him this week?

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
The week after a major is special.

This week: CJ Cup Byron Nelson at TPC Craig Ranch, McKinney, TX.

Scheffler defends (won at 31-under last year). Spieth is home. Aaron Rai plays his first event as a major champion.

Starts Thursday. 🏌️

#GolfFanrecap #ByronNelson

article.headline: CJ Cup Byron Nelson Preview: Scheffler Defends as Rai Arrives as a Champion

stories.title: Who Is Aaron Rai? The Humble Champion Nobody Saw Coming
stories.angle: The human story behind the numbers: a quietly determined golfer who proved that persistence and process matter more than pedigree.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Before this week, Aaron Rai was known for two things:

→ Wearing two gloves
→ Winning the 2024 Wyndham Championship

Now add a third: 2026 PGA CHAMPION.

The 31-year-old from Wombourne, England. At 290-1. With a 70-foot putt on 17.

That's the story. 🏆

#GolfFanrecap #AaronRai

article.headline: Who Is Aaron Rai? The Story Behind Golf's Most Unlikely Major Champion
